title: Inverse-perp users get reconstruct_complete with $0 equity (no audit signal at handler level)
summary: When _resolve_perp_amt_base returns INVERSE_UNSUPPORTED, _compute_daily_equity unconditionally continues (skips the fill) and never accumulates position state for the inverse perp. A user whose ONLY trades are inverse perps (BTC/USD:BTC on Bybit/OKX) gets a fully-replayed series of $0 daily rows. count>0 ⇒ audit_kind = reconstruct_complete (not reconstruct_unexpected_noop), so operators only see the inflated rows count and the inverse_perp_symbols telemetry list. There's no top-level audit kind (e.g. reconstruct_partial_unsupported) that flags 'all activity was discarded'. The dashboard renders a flat $0 curve as if the account were empty — exactly the V-shape silent-failure pattern v0.15.3.x was trying to retire.
evidence: equity_reconstruction.py:913-925 — `if amt_src == _PerpAmtSource.INVERSE_UNSUPPORTED: ... continue` skips the fill. equity_reconstruction.py:1818-1823 — audit_kind only checks `count == 0 and rows`, so non-zero rows with $0 totals stamp `reconstruct_complete`.
fix: At the handler level (run_reconstruct_allocator_history_job), after replay returns, check whether telemetry.inverse_perp_symbols is non-empty AND every row total is 0 (or rows total is suspiciously close to 0 vs anchor). If so, stamp `allocator.equity.reconstruct_partial_unsupported` (or similar) and surface in DispatchResult so the dashboard can render a 'reconstruct unsupported for this account shape' state instead of a flat-line curve. At minimum, when inverse_perp_symbols is non-empty AND len(spot+linear-perp activity)==0, the handler should DispatchOutcome.FAILED with error_kind='permanent' rather than DONE.

title: asyncio.sleep swallow narrowed but won't surface real failures
summary: _rate_limit_sleep and the CoinGecko-throttle sleep both narrowed their swallow from `except Exception` to `except (TypeError, ValueError)`. The narrow set is fine for letting CancelledError propagate, but TypeError/ValueError from `await asyncio.sleep(float(ms)/1000.0)` is essentially unreachable in production (ms is already guarded as int|float >0 on line 403). The audit value of the warning log is near zero — but more importantly, any OTHER asyncio exception (RuntimeError from a closed loop, MemoryError, etc.) now bubbles past the swallow, which is the *intended* behaviour for cancellation but means a transient runtime issue mid-sleep will now propagate to the outer except-block and re-classify the entire reconstruct as FAILED. That's a behaviour change that callers may not have anticipated.
evidence: equity_reconstruction.py:405-408 — `await asyncio.sleep(...)` followed by `except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc: logger.warning(...)`. Same pattern at lines 648-651 (CoinGecko sleep) with `pass` instead of logger.warning, so a sibling narrowing inconsistency: one logs, the other is silent.
fix: Either (a) drop both try blocks entirely (asyncio.sleep with a validated positive float can't raise TypeError/ValueError under cpython), or (b) align the two swallow handlers — both should log so behaviour is symmetric. The line-651 pass-only swallow is a textbook silent-skip pattern even if the narrow set makes it unlikely to fire.

title: sibling_check referenced after try/except — NameError on early-bail paths
summary: The audit-metadata block at line 1825-1844 references `sibling_check.lookup_failed` outside the try/except that ASSIGNS sibling_check. The variable is only bound if the inner block at line 1766 (`sibling_check = await _allocator_has_other_api_keys(...)`) completed without raising. If `_allocator_has_other_api_keys` somehow raises (it shouldn't — it has its own broad except — but a BaseException-derived exception, an attribute lookup failure on supabase, or an asyncio.CancelledError will bypass its `except Exception` and bubble), the outer except catches it and returns FAILED at line 1808 — so the line-1834 reference never executes in that path. BUT if any future refactor reorders or moves the audit emission before the try-cover, NameError will fire silently as a fresh Exception swallowed by the outer except. This is fragile coupling between two blocks separated by ~70 lines of code.
evidence: equity_reconstruction.py:1764 outer try, 1766 sibling_check assigned, 1792 except returns, 1834 sibling_check.lookup_failed referenced WITHOUT a guard.
fix: Initialise `sibling_check = SiblingCheckResult(has_siblings=False)` before the try block (or thread it through the audit-emit path via the success-return). Even better, hoist the audit emission INTO the try block so a sibling_check assignment failure cannot reach the metadata builder.

title: Refresh-path missing-unrealized_pnl_usd audit fires per-symbol — floods audit_events
summary: When a derivative holding row has `unrealized_pnl_usd IS NULL`, the refresh job emits an `allocator.equity.perp_upnl_missing` audit event INSIDE the per-symbol loop. An account with N stale derivative rows (NULL upnl) produces N audit events on every daily refresh, indefinitely, until the poll job repopulates the field. The pre-fix `float(... or 0.0)` then `continue if upnl == 0` was wrong-but-bounded; the new behaviour is correct-but-unbounded and will inflate audit_events table size for any allocator with a stuck poller. There's no de-dup, no aggregation, no rate limit.
evidence: equity_reconstruction.py:1907-1919 — per-symbol `_emit_audit(... 'allocator.equity.perp_upnl_missing', {symbol, venue})` inside a `for h in holdings:` loop with no aggregation.
fix: Collect missing-upnl symbols into a list during the loop and emit ONE `perp_upnl_missing` audit at the end of the handler with the full symbol list (mirroring the telemetry pattern used in reconstruct). Alternatively, dedupe with a per-handler-run set so the same (allocator, key, symbol) tuple emits at most once per refresh run.

title: Ctval-table override (drift > 5%) is the success branch but emits no observability signal
summary: When the defensive override actually fires (`relative_err > 0.05` ⇒ amt_src=CTVAL_TABLE), it silently rescues the value with no logger.warning and no telemetry append. The drift warning at the call site only fires for the SUB-threshold band (0.01–0.05) — the actual-override case crosses the threshold and emits nothing. This means: when ccxt fails to apply contractSize across the board (the 100x inflation bug the override defends against), we'd see no signal in audit_events that the override fired N hundred times. The audit log would look normal while the override is doing all the work — and a stale ctVal entry could under-correct without leaving a breadcrumb.
evidence: equity_reconstruction.py:328-330 — `if relative_err > PERP_AMT_CTVAL_DIVERGENCE_THRESHOLD: return amt_explicit, _PerpAmtSource.CTVAL_TABLE, relative_err` — caller at 947-963 only logs drift in the 0.01–0.05 band, the > 0.05 override returns silently.
fix: In _compute_daily_equity, also record an observability signal when amt_src == CTVAL_TABLE (e.g. add to a `ctval_override_fired` list/counter). This separates 'override never needed' (healthy) from 'override fires routinely' (ccxt safe_trade broken or table stale).

title: Bybit SWAP fills bypass the ctVal table and ride raw cost/price with no observability
summary: The venue gate at line 308-309 returns Bybit (and every non-OKX venue) SWAP fills as `COST_DIV_PRICE` with drift=None. The caller's unknown_perp_symbols check at line 926-946 requires `venue=='okx'`, so Bybit SWAPs with broken cost values (the exact symptom that motivated the OKX defensive override) are never surfaced. A Bybit allocator could have cost=amount*price (no contractSize applied) and the replay would silently inflate their equity by the contract multiplier with zero audit signal. The docstring at line 176-180 (`The OKX_PERP_CONTRACT_SIZE table is OKX-specific... avoid applying OKX ctVal values to a Bybit trade`) is correct on the safety side, but the absence of any Bybit-equivalent table or surfacing means we know there's a coverage gap and we silently accept it.
evidence: equity_reconstruction.py:308-309 (Bybit gate) and 926-946 (caller-side filter requires okx); commit comment at H-1158/M-1022 acknowledges the gap but only addresses the safety direction (don't apply OKX table to Bybit) without surfacing the data quality risk.
fix: Emit a separate telemetry signal `bybit_unverified_perp_symbols` (or similar) when amt_src==COST_DIV_PRICE and inst_type SWAP/FUTURES and venue!='okx', so an operator at least sees that we're relying on cost/price for venues where we have no defensive cover. This mirrors the existing C-0329 unknown_perp_symbols signal.
